title: Gulf funds place multi-billion orders for SpaceX IPO
subtitle: Gulf wealth funds have placed orders worth several billion dollars for SpaceX's upcoming IPO, according to people familiar with the matter. The demand signals Middle East investors' continued appetite for funding the global AI buildout.

title: Cyera’s $12B bet on AI trust
subtitle: Cyera raised $600 million at a $12 billion valuation, bringing its total funding above $2 billion. The round cements the data security startup's status as one of the most valuable private cybersecurity firms worldwide.

title: Zest bets credit card data can replace the restaurant wishlist
subtitle: Zest, backed by Alexis Ohanian's 776 and Kindred Ventures, has launched a restaurant discovery app that uses transaction data and AI to recommend restaurants based on users' actual dining habits. The approach aims to surface spots people genuinely frequent rather than relying on reviews or rankings.

title: Sequent launches to automate ASI alignment verification
subtitle: Sequent is building automated tools to verify AI alignment before training superintelligence. With ASI potentially arriving in years rather than decades, current empirical safety programs at AI labs may not deliver sufficient pre-training confidence.

title: Apollo's Zito calls most AI spending low-IQ
subtitle: Apollo Global Management's John Zito said many companies are deploying AI for mundane tasks that don't justify the costs and computing power. His remarks add to Wall Street skepticism that massive AI spending will translate into real returns.

title: Howard's AI freeze proposal targets Anthropic
subtitle: Jeremy Howard suggests the top-ranked AI lab should agree not to use its leading model for frontier AI development, while still sharing it with everyone else. This would freeze the frontier by definition, preventing recursive self-improvement races.

title: NEURA's $1.4B is really a bet on robot training gyms
subtitle: NEURA Robotics secured up to $1.4 billion in Series C funding from investors including Tether, Qualcomm, Amazon, and NVIDIA. The capital accelerates development of its physical AI and cognitive robotics platform.

title: Anthropic CEO: no idea if Claude killed 120 children in Iran strike
subtitle: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei says he doesn't know if Claude was used in an Iranian missile strike that killed an estimated 120 children. The admission exposes accountability gaps as AI firms sell tools to the US military.

title: Europe bets on enterprise AI over chatbots
subtitle: VivaTech 2026 will spotlight enterprise AI applications, with European companies emphasizing AI for complex existing systems over consumer products. This marks a strategic divergence from Silicon Valley's heavy investment in large language models.

title: Tailscale on an iPad broke a peer-to-peer app for two weeks
subtitle: A developer traced a stubborn WebRTC connection issue to an iPad running Tailscale, which was intercepting local network traffic. Client-side VPNs on devices can disrupt peer-to-peer connections in unexpected ways.

title: AWS ships Graviton5, and the x86 case keeps thinning
subtitle: AWS launched EC2 M9g and M9gd instances powered by Graviton5 processors, claiming 25% better compute performance than Graviton4. The chips target general-purpose and storage-optimized workloads, giving customers more performance per watt.

title: SpaceX IPO stress-tests Wall Street plumbing
subtitle: Wall Street banks are preparing the backend infrastructure for SpaceX's IPO on Friday, which could become the largest IPO in history. The focus has shifted from valuing the company to ensuring trading systems can handle the demand.
